stories.name: Information
stories.snippet: const Information = () => <Alert headline="Info headline" body="Alert body" variant="information" />;

variant.description:
Variant of the alert. Each color is reserved for its purpose and must not be used elsewhere.
- `error` (red) — High criticality. The user is completely stopped.
- `warning` (yellow) — Medium criticality. Some parts are still usable.
- `information` (purple) — Low criticality. Informational; everything still works.
- `success` (green) — Action completed successfully.
- `superVisible` (dark red) — Extra emphasis when "message"/"subdued" don't stand out enough. Do not overuse.
- `message` — General-purpose message.
- `subdued` — Lower visual weight message.
- `formMessage` — Used for form-level messages.
- `waiting` — Indicates a pending/loading state.
variant.name: variant

inline.description: Determines if the alert should be displayed inline, i.e without a min width
inline.name: inline

link.description: Decide if you want to show a link at the end of the alert
link.name: link
